generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR providing spatial perception data for real-time obstacle detection and environment mapping in autonomous vehicle applications. Operates at frequencies sufficient for real-time processing (exact frequency not specified)."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing depth perception data for 3D environment reconstruction and object detection in autonomous vehicle applications. Supports real-time data fusion with LiDAR for enhanced spatial awareness."
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor system collecting real-time vehicle state metrics including speed, acceleration, and trajectory for adaptive cruise control (ACC) applications. Interfaces with ESC telemetry and steering systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

instance ElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller (ESC) providing motor voltage commands and feedback for vehicle actuation. Collects real-time motor state data for synchronization with digital twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ActuationFeedbackData
]

instance SteeringServo : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo system providing real-time steering angle and position data for autonomous vehicle control. Enables precise trajectory following and collision avoidance in dynamic environments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringPositionData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw and pre-processed LiDAR point cloud data transmitted from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR to Digital Twin Entity. Includes 2D point representations converted into structured object tracks via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking component."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Depth perception data from Intel RealSense D435i camera transmitted to Digital Twin Entity for real-time environment reconstruction and object detection. Fused with LiDAR data for enhanced spatial awareness."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iDepthCamera
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time vehicle state metrics including speed, acceleration, and trajectory collected by Vehicle State Sensor and transmitted to Digital Twin Entity for synchronization with physical twin."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance ActuationFeedbackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Motor voltage commands and feedback from Electronic Speed Controller transmitted to Digital Twin Entity for actuation synchronization and control loop validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ElectronicSpeedController
]

instance SteeringPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time steering angle and position data from Steering Servo transmitted to Digital Twin Entity for trajectory synchronization and collision avoidance validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServo
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Software component converting raw LiDAR point cloud data into structured object tracks and ego-vehicle state information. Enables real-time visualization and analysis of vehicle surroundings."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateAggregationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data aggregation model collecting and processing real-time vehicle state metrics (speed, acceleration, trajectory) for synchronization with digital twin representations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance LiDARPointCloudDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured object tracks and ego-vehicle state information derived from raw LiDAR point cloud data via Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking model. Used for real-time visualization and control decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance DepthCameraProcessedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth perception data from Intel RealSense D435i camera fused with LiDAR data for enhanced 3D environment reconstruction. Used for object detection and spatial awareness in autonomous driving."
    DTDFVocab:fromData DepthCameraData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance VehicleStateProcessedData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated and validated vehicle state metrics including speed, acceleration, and trajectory for real-time monitoring and control synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ActuationCommandData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed actuation commands and feedback data for motor and steering systems, ensuring synchronization between physical and digital twin states."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ActuationFeedbackData, SteeringPositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance SensorLogsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured storage of sensor logs, system states, and operational reports in SQL database for historical analysis and reporting. Supports querying and long-term data retention."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2) serving as primary communication and service orchestration layer. Enables modularity, message passing, and real-time data exchange between components with 100ms target response time for critical control loops."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonEdgePlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson hardware platform providing GPU acceleration for sensor data processing and control algorithms at the edge. Supports real-time computation for autonomous vehicle applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance Python38Scripting : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 scripting environment for implementing control algorithms, data processing pipelines, and ROS2-based interfaces. Aligned with ISO 23247-1:2021 standards for model-based design."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance MATLABSimulink : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for model-based design and simulation of Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) system. Ensures alignment with ISO 23247-1:2021 standards for digital twin development."
    DTDFVocab:enables ComputationOffloadingService
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for structured storage of sensor logs, system states, and operational reports. Supports querying, historical analysis, and long-term data retention for digital twin applications."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService
]

instance ROS2NativeCommunication : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-native topics and services for intra-system communication, eliminating need for external brokers like MQTT or Kafka. Supports real-time data exchange with 100ms latency constraints."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeDataMonitoringService, ComputationOffloadingService, RemoteManagementService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time viewing, saving, and generating reports of sensor data (LiDAR, depth camera) and system states (ESC telemetry) via ROS2-based interface. Supports latency-constrained operations with target response time of 100ms for critical control loops."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeSensorVisualizationInsight, SystemStateReportingInsight
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating remote processing of computationally intensive tasks (path planning, obstacle detection) via edge-cloud collaboration. Reduces onboard load while maintaining real-time performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides PathPlanningInsight, ObstacleDetectionInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service allowing remote enabling/disabling of ACC functionality, parameter tuning, and system configuration adjustments through user-friendly dashboard integrated with ROS2 services. Supports basic fault handling (system reset, mode switching)."
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemConfigurationInsight, FaultHandlingAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeSensorVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D visualization of vehicle surroundings using processed LiDAR and depth camera data for operator awareness and decision support."
]

instance SystemStateReportingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured reports of vehicle system states, sensor logs, and operational metrics for historical analysis and performance monitoring."
]

instance PathPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Optimized path planning trajectories generated through remote computation offloading, considering real-time sensor data and dynamic environment constraints."
]

instance ObstacleDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time detection and classification of obstacles in vehicle surroundings using fused sensor data (LiDAR, depth camera) for collision avoidance."
]

instance SystemConfigurationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current system configuration parameters including ACC functionality status, control thresholds, and operational modes for remote management."
]

instance FaultHandlingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic execution of basic fault handling procedures including system reset and mode switching to maintain operational safety."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance ElectronicSpeedControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Vedder Electronic Speed Controller converting control commands into motor voltage commands for vehicle propulsion. Provides real-time feedback for synchronization with digital twin representations."
]

instance SteeringServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo system executing precise steering angle commands for autonomous vehicle trajectory control. Enables collision avoidance and adaptive cruise control functionality."
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is governed by the **Controlling FE (ACC)**, which processes digital twin data (e.g., lead vehicle detection, projected driving paths) to generate control commands for the physical vehicle. The **Controlling FE** computes desired vehicle speed based on adaptive cruise control (ACC) logic and transmits these commands to the **Actuation FE**, which converts them into **motor voltage commands** for execution. The **Data Translation FE** ensures command serialization and unit alignment before transmission. Communication between the **Digital Twin Entity** and **Physical Twin Entity** occurs over a **Wi-Fi network with UDP transport**, while intra-entity control signals propagate via **ROS2**. No explicit validation, authentication, or conflict resolution mechanisms are described, though the **Data Translation FE** likely enforces message integrity. Command execution confirmation is not detailed, but the system assumes closed-loop feedback via sensor data reprocessing (e.g., LiDAR tracking)."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination, adhering to hardware limitations. This temporal granularity is critical for safety-critical vehicular applications, where stricter synchronization (e.g., sub-100 ms) is mandated by many standards. The system does not explicitly specify varying time scales for different services or latency requirements beyond this synchronization constraint, though the **ROS2-based architecture** (via individual ROS2 packages) implies real-time or near-real-time execution for sensor-actuator loops and data processing within the Physical Twin Entity. No explicit mention of asynchronous or event-driven temporal models is provided, but the **entity-based decomposition** (e.g., User Entity, Digital Twin Entity, Physical Twin Entity) suggests modular time-critical services with implicit dependencies on synchronized data exchange."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS follows an **ISO 23247 Reference Architecture (RA)-inspired, entity-centric orchestration pattern**, where software components are **mapped to Functional Entities (FEs)** and deployed as **independent ROS2 packages**. Each package encapsulates one or more ROS2 nodes implementing the FE’s functionality, enabling modular execution on heterogeneous hardware (e.g., edge devices for the Physical Twin Entity, user-facing systems for the User Entity). Key deviations from ISO 23247 include:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity expansion**: Hosts general automotive functions (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) beyond resource-specific operations, requiring redefined ownership and engineering processes.
  2. **User Interface FE split**: Divided into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* subcomponents within the User Entity.
  3. **Data Storage FE misalignment**: No direct mapping to an ISO 23247 FE, indicating an ad-hoc integration.
  Coordination is achieved via **ROS2’s distributed communication model** (topics, services) and the **100 ms synchronized clock**, with no explicit mention of higher-level orchestration (e.g., Kubernetes, middleware brokers). Resource management is implicit, tied to the entity’s hardware deployment (e.g., compute/sensor/actuator resources in the Physical Twin Entity)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle stages, as implied by the ISO 23247 standard and related context, encompass at least two primary phases: **design phase** and **vehicle operation phase**. During the **design phase**, the DT serves as a product lifecycle management (PLM) tool, enabling virtual modeling, simulation, and validation of manufacturing elements (e.g., equipment, facilities, products, processes) before physical deployment. During **vehicle operation**, the DT continues to represent the Observable Manufacturing Element (OME) in real-time, facilitating monitoring, diagnostics, and adaptive control. The transition between phases involves **architectural design** and **implementation** of the DT system, as outlined in the engineering steps, where requirements are defined, and the DT is mapped to its physical counterpart. The **Digital Twin Entity** (DTE) dynamically evolves alongside the OME, ensuring synchronization through data exchange protocols (e.g., OPC UA). Lifecycle governance is not explicitly detailed but is inferred to rely on the **entity-based Reference Architecture (RA)**, where each entity (OME, Data Collection and Device Control, DTE, User) operates autonomously yet collaboratively to maintain consistency across phases. No explicit governance frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 15288) or transition protocols are specified in the provided documents."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The document does not explicitly describe **fidelity requirements** (e.g., model granularity, resolution) or **validation and verification (V&V) methods** for the Digital Twin system. However, the **ISO 23247 standard** emphasizes **real-time operational fidelity**, with constraints such as **network latency of ≤100 ms** (R7) and **clock synchronization accuracy of ≤100 ms** (R8) to ensure temporal consistency. The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity** is tasked with collecting data from the OME, but no mechanisms for **uncertainty quantification**, **error detection**, or **quality assurance** are specified. The **User Entity** provides monitoring interfaces, but no validation protocols for model accuracy or sensor data integrity are documented."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin System’s Technical Connection** infrastructure is designed to support real-time data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity** with minimal latency and high reliability. Key technical aspects include:

**Network Topology and Architecture**:
- **Intra-Entity Communication**: Software components within each entity (Physical, Digital, User) communicate via a **ROS2 network**, a middleware framework enabling modular, publish-subscribe-based data exchange.
- **Inter-Entity Communication**: Data transmission between entities occurs over a **Wi-Fi network**, with the transport layer protocol unspecified (text cuts off at 'using U', but UDP is implied for low-latency requirements).
- **Three-Tier Architecture**: The system follows a hierarchical structure with clear separation between:
  - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sensor data acquisition (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo, vehicle state).
  - **Digital Twin Entity**: Data processing, visualization, and control logic.
  - **User Entity**: Human-machine interface (e.g., 3D visualization, control panel).

**Communication Protocols**:
- **ROS2**: Used for intra-entity communication, providing topics and services for dynamic data exchange between software components (e.g., **Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking**, **Actuation FE**, **Controlling FE**).
- **Wi-Fi (UDP)**: Likely employed for inter-entity communication due to real-time constraints, though the exact protocol is not fully specified.

**Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
- The system must maintain a **network latency of at most 100 ms** to ensure real-time responsiveness for control and visualization tasks.
- Bandwidth requirements are not explicitly quantified but are inferred to be sufficient for streaming LiDAR point clouds, vehicle state data, and control commands without significant delay.

**Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
- **Data Translation FE**: Ensures data integrity through serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion, reducing the risk of communication errors.
- **ROS2 Quality of Service (QoS)**: Likely configured to prioritize real-time data streams (e.g., LiDAR, vehicle state) over less time-sensitive data (e.g., reports).
- **Redundancy**: Not explicitly detailed, but the modular ROS2 architecture allows for component replication or failover mechanisms to maintain system availability.

**Security Protocols**:
- Security measures are not explicitly described in the document. However, the use of **Data Translation FE** for message validation and the reliance on standardized protocols (ROS2, Wi-Fi) suggest inherent security through data integrity checks and encryption (if implemented at the transport layer).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin System’s hosting and deployment** are distributed across **three distinct hardware components**, each hosting one of the three entities (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User). The **Physical Twin Entity** runs on onboard vehicle hardware, while the **Digital Twin Entity** is deployed on a separate device (likely an **NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano** or equivalent edge platform) to handle computationally intensive tasks like **LiDAR clustering, Kalman filtering, and 3D visualization**. The **User Entity** operates remotely, enabling visualization and control via a **3D cockpit interface** (Fig. 3b) and **Control Panel FE**, with data storage for ego vehicle state managed externally (e.g., cloud or edge storage). No explicit scalability mechanisms or redundancy strategies are detailed, though the separation of entities suggests modularity. The system does not specify cloud hosting but implies **edge deployment** for low-latency processing."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin (DT) system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines an **entity-based framework** for manufacturing DTs. Key compliance aspects include:
  - **Industry Standard**: ISO 23247 (2021), structured into four parts (Overview, Reference Architecture, Digital Representation, Information Exchange).
  - **Reference Architecture**: An **entity-based model** with four core entities (*Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)*, *Data Collection and Device Control Entity*, *Digital Twin Entity*, *User Entity*), each composed of **Functional Entities (FEs)** for modular functionality.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: The standard does not mandate specific protocols but allows flexibility (e.g., **OPC Unified Architecture** for data exchange).
  - **Certification Requirements**: Not explicitly mentioned, though adherence to ISO 23247 implies compliance with manufacturing DT best practices.
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: The architecture ensures alignment with **manufacturing-specific digital twin requirements**, though broader compliance (e.g., cybersecurity, data privacy) is not detailed.
  The standard emphasizes **modularity** and **interoperability** to facilitate integration with other systems, but no additional certification or compliance frameworks (e.g., IEC 62443 for cybersecurity) are referenced."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, mandating performance requirements and test procedures to ensure safety-critical operation. The system also aligns with **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 (Release 15)** for **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) service requirements**, specifying technical specifications for secure communication protocols in safety-critical transport scenarios. No explicit mention of cybersecurity protocols (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection), access control mechanisms, or threat mitigation strategies is provided in the documents. The system is implicitly safety-critical due to its alignment with ISO 15622, but no direct references to **ISO 26262-1:2018** (functional safety) or **Dalibor et al. (2020)** are found."
]
